title: delphinium tenuisectum subsp. amplibracteatum
intro: Delphinium tenuisectum subsp. amplibracteatum is a subspecies of the Delphinium genus, commonly known as the larkspur or delphinium, which is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the world. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to induce perspiration and reduce fever, as well as its antispasmodic and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Delphinium tenuisectum subsp. amplibracteatum include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
